name: Engineered Civilization
summary: A path for people who want to understand and build the systems that modern life depends on: technology, infrastructure, AI, energy, aerospace, and operations.
thesis: Learn how complex systems are designed, run, and improved in the real world.
fitSignals: Likes systems, tools, and real-world applications
Wants to understand infrastructure and technology as lived civilization
Feels motivated by building and operating useful things
Prefers practical examples with enough theory to be credible

defaults.reviewRhythm: Two deep sessions and two practical sessions per week
defaults.milestoneTemplate: Use {coreSubject} and {supportingTopic} to build something concrete through the {role} lens

weeklyCadencePresets.gentle: 2 study blocks and 1 hands-on session each week
weeklyCadencePresets.balanced: 4 sessions per week with one project review
weeklyCadencePresets.ambitious: 5 to 6 sessions per week with active implementation
weeklyCadencePresets.obsessive: Daily technical work with a standing project queue
